_meta.description: All voice lines for Golf Mind Game, organized by voice and category. Each line has an ElevenLabs-ready prompt with emotion/delivery direction in angle brackets.
_meta.usage: Feed each 'prompt' to ElevenLabs TTS with the corresponding voice from voice-manifest.json

hole_reactions.prompt: <impressed, genuine> That's how you play that hole.
<casual, encouraging> Solid par. On to the next one.
<sympathetic shrug> Bogey's not the end of the world. Plenty of golf left.
<stunned> No way. No. Way. Did that just happen?
<jaw drop> Oh my god. That's... that's a bomb.
<trying not to react> ...yep. Alright. We'll find it.

between_holes_mood.prompt: <beaming, slap on back> Man, you are ON today. I should play with you more often — you're good luck.
<laughing> You know what, I'm buying lunch after this.
<casual, warm> Nice playing back there. Keep it up.
<offering> Not bad at all. Want a granola bar? Peanut butter chocolate.
<slightly tense> Maybe pick up the pace a little?
<on phone, distant> Yeah... yeah, might wrap up early.

dialogue_tree_lines.prompt: <impressed> Not a bad start. Most guys come out here and hack it around the first hole. You looked pretty calm out there.
<reassuring> Shake it off. First hole at Pebble Beach — everyone's nervous. The course opens up from here.
<genuine laugh> Yeah, I get it. My first time here I topped my opening tee shot. Like, topped it. Went maybe forty yards.
<skeptical half-smile> Visualizing? Like, with your eyes closed and everything?
<surprised respect> Huh. You actually know this course.
<eyes wide> Driver? On four? It's 327 yards. The green is the size of your living room.
<pause, then respect> ...alright. Let's see it then.
<soft laugh> Hey, I lost two balls here my first time. Two. Just aim at the center of the green and make a swing.
<respect, slight surprise> I like that attitude. Most people freeze up here.
<analytical> Left to right, maybe 10-15 miles per hour. See the flag? It's stiff. Aim left edge and let the wind bring it back.
<serious, weight of moment> This is it. The eighth at Pebble Beach. I don't care what your handicap is — this tee shot is going to make your heart race.
<nervous laugh> You're alive! I was ready to call your family.
<reflective, vulnerable> This is my third time. Second was with my dad, right before his knee surgery. He couldn't finish. We made it through seven.
<genuinely moved, clears throat> Yeah. Yeah, it's been solid.
<emotional but composed> You know what — same. This has been one of the best rounds I've ever played. Not the score. The round.

scene_setting.prompt: <quiet wisdom> A round of golf is not played on the course. It is played in your mind.
<setting the scene, unhurried> You are standing in the parking lot at Pebble Beach. The Pacific stretches to the horizon. The air smells like salt and freshly cut grass.
<building atmosphere> The morning mist hasn't quite burned off yet. You stand on the first tee of Pebble Beach, the most famous opening hole in American golf.
<awe, slight tension> The fifth at Pebble Beach. 188 yards, but every one of those yards is over a chasm.
<reverence> One hundred and six yards. The shortest hole at Pebble Beach, and arguably the most photographed hole in golf.
<gravity> Eight at Pebble Beach. This is the one. The signature hole.
<reflective, warm> The front nine at Pebble Beach is behind you. The ocean doesn't care what you shot. The cypress trees have seen worse.

transitions.prompt: <calm transition> You and Dave walk off the first green toward the second tee. The mist is lifting.
<wonder> Walking to the third tee, you catch your first real glimpse of the ocean through the pines.
<heavy anticipation> The walk to eight is short and heavy with anticipation. This is the hole you see on television.
<wind down> Eight is behind you. The chasm is behind you. Whatever the scorecard says, you hit a shot over the Pacific Ocean.

ambient.prompt: <gentle> The waves break against the rocks below.
<whisper> Silence. Just the ocean and the grass.

swing_thoughts.prompt: <internal, rising tension> You begin your backswing. Your mind starts to wander...
<anxious> At the top of your backswing, a thought arrives uninvited...
<dreamy, drifting> That waitress at the turn had incredible eyes. What was her name? Something with a K...
<panicking, getting faster> Don't go left. Don't go left. Don't go left. DON'T go left.
<shaky> You notice your hands are shaking. Are they shaking? Look at them. They're shaking.
<spiraling> What's the etiquette here — do I mark this? Is Dave expecting me to mark? He's standing on my line.
<tense> Your boss is playing behind you. He mentioned this morning he's restructuring the department. Don't think about it.
<enchanted, slowing down> You make eye contact with a sea otter floating in the cove below. It's on its back, eating a clam. Living its best life.
<hyped, reckless> Hit it as hard as you possibly can. This is Pebble Beach. You might never be back. Grip it and rip it.
<dizzy, unsteady> I wonder how deep that water is. Like, if I jumped. Not in a dark way. Just... it's really far down, right?
<spiraling> What happens if I don't clear it? Like, actually? Is that ball gone forever?
<slow, deliberate breathing> Inhale. Exhale. Feel your feet on the ground. This is just another shot.

perk_unlocks.prompt: <clarity> Something clicks. Your mind feels... sharper.
<confident> You feel it. That energy. You belong here.
<amused> You realize you're actually having fun. Like, genuinely.
<analytical, satisfied> The course makes sense now. You can see the architect's intent.

multiplayer.prompt: <PA system> Now on the course: a new player has joined.
<PA system> Attention on the course. Players approaching.
<PA system> Your tee time is now. Please proceed to the first tee.
